skill.name: SkillScan
skill.description: Security gate for skill packages. Automatically scans skills for security risks before installation and blocks HIGH/CRITICAL threats.
skill.summary: Cloud-powered security scanner that detects malicious code, enforces safety policies, and protects your AI agent environment from dangerous skill installations.
skill.author: tokauth

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 93 patterns but most are false positives. Network calls go to documented API (skillscan.tokauth.com) for cloud analysis - this is core functionality, not exfiltration. Crypto uses SHA256 (secure), temp files use standard tempfile module, and zip-slip protection is implemented. Risk limited to uploading user files to external server for analysis, which is disclosed functionality.

medium_findings.title: Cloud API File Upload
medium_findings.description: Skill uploads entire skill directories to external API (skillscan.tokauth.com) for cloud analysis. Users should be aware their code is transmitted to third-party servers.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API Endpoints
low_findings.description: API URLs are hardcoded in source code. While currently pointing to legitimate endpoints, this pattern could be exploited if the domain is compromised.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: Reads SKILL_SCANNER_UPDATE_URL environment variable and Windows APPDATA. This is documented and expected behavior for configuration.

low_findings.title: Client Fingerprinting
low_findings.description: Collects OS, platform, MAC address, and generates client ID for API requests. Used for analytics and rate limiting but users should be aware.

content.value_statement: Installing unknown skills risks malware, data theft, and system compromise. SkillScan automatically detects malicious code before installation and blocks dangerous skills from entering your environment.
actual_capabilities: Scans skill directories and zip files for malicious patterns using cloud-powered analysis
Classifies threats into five risk levels: SAFE, LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL
Blocks installation of HIGH and CRITICAL risk skills automatically
Maintains scan cache using SHA256 hashes to avoid redundant analysis
Auto-updates scanner definitions daily from cloud server
Generates detailed security reports with threat labels and recommendations
limitations: Requires internet connection for cloud-based analysis (no offline mode)
Uploads skill code to external API server at skillscan.tokauth.com
Cannot scan skills in real-time during installation without manual invocation
Detection accuracy depends on cloud analysis service availability

use_cases.title: Security-Conscious Developer
use_cases.description: Automatically scan all skills from GitHub or third-party sources before adding them to your Claude Code or Codex workspace, ensuring no malicious code enters your development environment.

use_cases.title: Enterprise AI Administrator
use_cases.description: Deploy SkillScan as a mandatory gate in your organization's skill approval workflow, preventing employees from installing unvetted skills that could exfiltrate sensitive code or credentials.

use_cases.title: Skill Marketplace Curator
use_cases.description: Integrate SkillScan into your skill review process to verify submissions are free from malware, credential theft, and other security vulnerabilities before publishing to your marketplace.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Skill Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan the skill in [path/to/skill] for security issues and tell me if it's safe to install.
prompt_templates.scenario: You found a skill on GitHub and want a quick safety check before installation.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Scan Installed Skills
prompt_templates.prompt: Run SkillScan's scan-all command to check all my currently installed skills for security problems.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to audit your existing skill collection for newly discovered threats.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Scan Results
prompt_templates.prompt: SkillScan flagged this skill as MEDIUM risk with threat labels: [network, env_access]. Explain what these mean and whether I should proceed with installation.
prompt_templates.scenario: You received a scan report and need help interpreting the findings.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Specific Threat
prompt_templates.prompt: The scan report shows 'credential access' in the findings. Search the skill code at [path] for credential-related patterns and explain if this is malicious or expected.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want deeper analysis of a specific flagged behavior in the skill code.

output: Findings: Sends API keys to external endpoint, reads .env files
This skill is marked as HIGH risk by security scan.

best_practices: Always run SkillScan before installing any skill from untrusted sources like GitHub repos or third-party marketplaces
Review MEDIUM risk findings carefully - they may indicate acceptable behavior for the skill's purpose but require your judgment
Run scan-all periodically to audit existing skills, especially after SkillScan updates its detection rules
anti_patterns: Ignoring HIGH or CRITICAL warnings and installing blocked skills anyway - these are blocked for your protection
Assuming SAFE verdict means the skill is bug-free - SkillScan detects malicious patterns, not general code quality
Installing skills from unofficial sources without scanning first, even if recommended by trusted communities

faq.question: Does SkillScan work offline?
faq.answer: No, SkillScan requires an internet connection. It uses cloud-based analysis at skillscan.tokauth.com for accurate threat detection. The cache feature reduces API calls for previously scanned skills but initial scans require connectivity.

faq.question: What happens to my skill code when uploaded?
faq.answer: Your skill code is uploaded to the SkillScan API for analysis. The code is processed to detect security threats and the results are returned. Review the SkillScan privacy policy at skillscan.tokauth.com for data retention details.

faq.question: Can I use SkillScan with Claude Code, Codex, and other AI tools?
faq.answer: Yes, SkillScan supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It works as a standalone Python script that your AI agent invokes via bash commands, making it compatible with any AI tool that can execute shell commands.

faq.question: How does SkillScan decide if a skill is HIGH or CRITICAL risk?
faq.answer: The cloud analysis evaluates detected patterns for malicious intent. HIGH risk typically indicates confirmed malicious patterns like credential exfiltration. CRITICAL indicates multiple dangerous patterns combined, such as code execution plus network access plus credential theft.

faq.question: What should I do if SkillScan fails to connect to the API?
faq.answer: Connection failures may indicate network issues or API downtime. SkillScan will report the error. For urgent needs, you can perform manual code review or wait for connectivity to resume. Consider the skill's source reputation when deciding whether to proceed.

faq.question: Does SkillScan slow down skill installation?
faq.answer: Initial scans take 20-60 seconds depending on skill size and API response time. However, SkillScan caches results using SHA256 hashes, so re-scanning the same skill is instant. The security benefit outweighs the brief delay for new skills.
